name: Barranco del Cabrerizo and Rupestrian Paintings of Navazo from Albarracín
name_original: Barranco del Cabrerizo y Pinturas Rupestres del Navazo desde Albarracín
region_intro: Hiking around Albarracín offers exploration of a diverse landscape within the Sierra de Albarracín mountains. The region is characterized by extensive pine forests, red sandstone rock formations, and the meandering Guadalaviar River. Geological features include varied sandstone, limestone, and calcareous rock, contributing to a varied terrain. The area also features historical elements like medieval walls and prehistoric rock paintings. Best hiking trails around Albarracín The most popular hiking route is Barranco del Cabrerizo and Rupestrian Paintings of Navazo from Albarracín, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 27 minutes to complete. This route leads to ancient Rupestrian Paintings, combining natural scenery with historical interest. Another top favourite among local hikers is Guadalaviar Riverside Walk, a moderate 1.6 miles (2.6 km) path. This trail offers peaceful strolls along the riverbanks amidst lush greenery. Local hikers also love the Albarracín River Walk, a 2.4 miles (3.8 km) trail leading through the Guadalaviar River valley, often completed in about 1 hour 8 minutes. This circular route provides views of the town's medieval walls and features stone paths and hanging bridges. Hiking around Albarracín is defined by pine forests, red sandstone formations, and river canyons. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging routes through rocky terrain. The routes in Albarracín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 280 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Albarracín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Albarracín?
A: There are over 35 hiking trails around Albarracín, catering to various skill levels. This includes 15 easy routes, 20 moderate trails, and 2 more challenging options for experienced hikers.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Albarracín?
A: Albarracín offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll encounter extensive pine forests, striking red sandstone rock formations, and the meandering Guadalaviar River with its picturesque canyons. The region also features varied geological formations, including limestone and calcareous rock, creating a visually rich hiking experience.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hikes in Albarracín?
A: Yes, Albarracín has several easy trails suitable for families. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974514546/albarracin-river-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albarracín River Walk&lt;/a&gt;, a 2.4-mile circular route that offers unique views of the town's medieval walls and the Guadalaviar River, featuring stone paths and hanging bridges. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979738329/dornaque-interpretation-center-pena-del-hierro-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dornaque Interpretation Center - Peña del Hierro Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I see ancient rock paintings on the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region is home to prehistoric Levantine rock paintings. You can explore these ancient artworks on trails within the Pinares de Rodeno Natural Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974570623/barranco-del-cabrerizo-and-rupestrian-paintings-of-navazo-from-albarracin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Cabrerizo and Rupestrian Paintings of Navazo from Albarracín&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate trail that specifically leads to these Rupestrian Paintings.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Albarracín?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974514546/albarracin-river-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albarracín River Walk&lt;/a&gt; is a well-known circular route that provides excellent views of the town and river. Another circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976369919/pena-de-la-cruz-circular-by-the-pinares-de-rodeno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Cruz – circular by the Pinares de Rodeno&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the unique red sandstone formations of the natural park.

Q: What historical landmarks can I discover while hiking near Albarracín?
A: Beyond the town's medieval walls, you can explore several historical landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/689101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albarracín Old Town&lt;/a&gt; itself is a highlight. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4543098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Doña Blanca Tower&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peracense Castle&lt;/a&gt; nearby. Additionally, a linear route follows a section of a Roman aqueduct between Gea de Albarracín and Albarracín, showcasing ancient engineering.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or lakes to visit near Albarracín?
A: Yes, the Sierra de Albarracín features several beautiful water features. You can find waterfalls like El Salto de San Pedro in El Vallecillo and the Cascada de Calomarde. For lakes, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/756481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Bezas&lt;/a&gt;, the largest in the Sierra, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arquillo de San Blas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrería Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable natural monument.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Albarracín?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 280 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique blend of historical charm and breathtaking wilderness, particularly the stunning red sandstone formations in Pinares de Rodeno and the peaceful riverside walks along the Guadalaviar.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Albarracín?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on every trail are not detailed, the Albarracín region is generally welcoming to outdoor enthusiasts and their pets. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural parks like Pinares de Rodeno, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

Q: What are the best viewpoints in the Albarracín area?
A: For spectacular panoramic views, the medieval walls surrounding Albarracín offer easy access to various vantage points. The Torre del Andador provides the highest viewpoint in town, while the Plaza Mayor also offers impressive vistas over the Guadalaviar River. Within the Pinares de Rodeno Natural Park, the Peñas Royas Viewpoint is renowned for its natural surroundings.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Guadalaviar River?
A: Yes, the Guadalaviar River is a central feature of the region's hiking. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974501011/guadalaviar-riverside-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalaviar Riverside Walk&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate path offering peaceful strolls along the riverbanks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974514546/albarracin-river-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albarracín River Walk&lt;/a&gt; also follows the river valley, providing unique perspectives of the town.
tips: Mirador del Cabrerizo is located near Abrigo de la Fuente del Cabrerizo.

The Bull Shelter of Prado del Navazo, also known as Toricos del Navazo, is one of the most prominent sites of Levantine Rock Art in the Sierra de Albarracín (Teruel), and is part of the complex declared a Site of Cultural Interest and World Heritage Site by UNESCO.
Naturalistic representations of large bovids are painted in a yellowish-white color, unique in Levantine rock art, which typically uses reddish tones.
Protected landscape in the Sierra de Albarracin Cultural Park. In the middle of the Pinar de Ródeno

The Peñas Royas Viewpoint is one of the most spectacular spots in the natural surroundings of Albarracín, located within the Pinares de Rodeno Natural Park in the province of Teruel. It is located just a few kilometers from Albarracín's historic center.

Recreational area with tables and grills surrounded by pine forests that make the place very pleasant in summer. It has parking next to the road and from here you can access all the cave paintings in the area by paths.

Shelter of the Archer of the Enclosed Ravines
